fields.description:
## Why
Audit OSS finding #6 bucket (versioning honesty): Python 3.13 has been the current stable for over a year, but the project tests/advertises only 3.11 and 3.12 — test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml matrices stop at 3.12, and pyproject classifiers list 3.11/3.12 only. For a published PyPI package that leaves 3.13 (the default interpreter in many new environments) untested/unadvertised.

## What / files (edit surfaces that actually exist)
- `.github/workflows/test.yml` (matrix @ ~L151 `python-version: ["3.11","3.12"]`) and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (matrix @ ~L123): add `"3.13"` to the Linux python-version list (keep macOS pinned to its single 3.11 entry — documented cost policy).
- `pyproject.toml`: add `"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13"` classifier (after L35) once the matrix is green. `requires-python = ">=3.11"` already admits 3.13; no change there.
- README supported-version wording: README states "Python ≥ 3.11" (L140) / "python3 (≥ 3.11)" (L193) — these are FLOOR statements already satisfied by 3.13, so no correction is required; optionally add a "(3.11–3.13 tested in CI)" clarifier at L140. NOTE: docs/api-stability.md is NOT edited — it contains no Python-version wording (nothing to update there).

## Local-interpreter note
The local dev interpreter in this worktree is Python 3.14, not 3.13, so the authoritative 3.13 green signal is the CI matrix job (Gerrit Verified). Local `make lint && make typecheck && make test` on 3.14 is run as additional evidence; if a genuine 3.13-specific blocker surfaces in CI, the alternative remedy is to document 3.13 as excluded and withhold the classifier (audit's stated fallback).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml Linux matrices include "3.13" and the 3.13 job runs green in CI
- [ ] pyproject classifiers include `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13`; `twine check` / build passes
- [ ] README supported-version statements remain consistent with the matrix (floor "≥ 3.11" retained; optional 3.11–3.13 clarifier)
- [ ] `make lint` green locally; CI 3.13 job green (authoritative) — or, if a real 3.13 blocker exists, it is documented and the classifier withheld
